# Page Title: Mechanical Engineering | IIT Jammu

### Department Vision

The Mechanical Engineering Department of IIT Jammu was
established in 2016 and is one of the three founding departments of the Institute.
The department strives to play a role in nation-building by training engineers in an
effort to transform them into future leaders. We conduct translational research to
enable us to surmount technological barriers so that our present and future needs
are addressed. The department offers B.Tech., M.Tech., and Ph.D. programs. It
currently has 19 faculty members, 6 staff members, and approximately 150
undergraduate students (I – IV year), 28 postgraduate students (I – II
year students of Thermal and Energy Systems Engineering), and 38 Ph.D. students.

Mechanical engineering is one of the oldest and most
fundamental disciplines. A mechanical engineer is well-versed in the basic
principles of dynamics, energy, and materials science and uses it to conceptualize,
analyze, design and build mechanical systems that serve the interests of mankind and
our planet. Hence, it is one of the most broad-ranging engineering specialities; its
scope encompasses power plants and airplanes, cell phones, and biomedical equipment,
to name a few. Its diversity also enables the skilled engineer to engage with other
fundamental disciplines, such as physics and mathematics. In a nutshell, the
discipline of mechanical engineering focuses on developing processes, systems, and
products by harnessing energy and power across many scales, viz., from the nanoscale
to the vast, from simple and yet elegant to complex systems. This enables its
practitioners to achieve technological improvements and forge new domains.

**Vision and Mission of the Department:**

**Vision of the Department**

Our vision is to build a strong reputation for our academic programs through
state-of-the-art experiential curriculum, research, and technology incubation hubs.
In this endeavour, the Department of Mechanical Engineering aims to be globally
recognized as a knowledge and technology creator.

**Mission of the Department**

- Nurture our graduates to become adaptive professionals and be successful in
engineering practices, research, and public services.
- Creating a knowledge base through research in the scientific and technological
aspects of mechanical engineering.
- Serving the society through innovation and excellence in teaching and research.
- Establish state-of-the-art laboratories in line with the government’s
vision, “Viksit Bharat” through innovation and research.

#### Abhay Sharma

An academic and research leader with over 20 years of distinguished service across premier institutions, including **IIT Jammu**, **IIT Hyderabad**, and **KU Leuven (QS World Rank #42)**and Industry. Adept at institution building from the ground up, policy development, strategic planning, and academic governance, with transformative roles as **Dean**, **National Coordinator**, and **Head of Section**.  Authored over 100 scientific publications in Journals of high impact factor and conferences of international repute and led multi-crore research and infrastructure projects. An advocate and practitioner for **NEP 2020**, **interdisciplinary innovation**, and **global academic collaboration**

##### Research Interest

Manufacturing, Additive Manufacturing, Material Joining

#### Ashutosh Bijalwan

Ashutosh Bijalwan is an Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. His research spans Applied Mechanics, with emphasis on Optimal Control, Computational Mechanics, and Numerical Methods, particularly applied to soft and living systems.

He earned his PhD in Applied Mathematics with Excellent Cum Laude from CIMNE–UPC, Barcelona, Spain, where he worked on structure-preserving numerical strategies for optimal control of mechanical systems. He completed his Master’s in Engineering Mechanics from the Applied Mechanics Department at IIT Delhi, where he received the Prof. B. Karunesh Memorial Award for his thesis on visco-hyperelastic analysis of the ACL.

He previously worked at Eaton and Schlumberger as a Modeling and Simulation Engineer and later as a Research Engineer (Wells) in the Geomechanics Group at ExxonMobil, where he conducted research in multiscale modeling, uncertainty quantification, and inverse problems related to the mechanics of hydraulic fracturing in unconventional reservoirs.

His current research explores structure-preserving and multiscale computational frameworks for soft and living systems, with applications in tissue morphogenesis, biomedical devices, and bioinspired soft robotics.

#### Samrat Rao

Thank you for visiting my webpage. I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu. My research interests are in the simulation and analysis of the fluid dynamics of heated and unheated turbulent plumes which are an ansatz of clouds and forest-fires. I am also collaborating with my colleagues in the domain of heat-transfer in the materials science domain. My core teaching interest is in the intellectually challenging field of thermo-fluids.

I have received my undergraduate (BE) degree from NMAMIT Mangalore in 2004, graduate degree (ME) from IIEST Shibpur in 2007 and doctoral (PhD) degree from IISc Bangalore in 2014; all of them are in Mechanical Engineering.

Please do drop an email if you are interested in fulfilling your higher education ambitions with me.

#### Shanmugadas K.P

**Propulsion and Energy Research Lab**

Our research group specializes in the dynamic fields of aerospace engineering and propulsion. Our primary mission is to develop next-generation micro gas turbine engine technology for energy production and aerospace applications.  Additionally, a key focus of our research is on the development of reusable liquid rocket engines, with a particular emphasis on utilizing throttleable METHALOX injectors.

**Brief Bio**

Dr. Shanmugadas, assistant professor within the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu, spearheads the Propulsion and Energy Lab, driving pioneering research and innovation related to liquid rocket engines and aero engines. With a primary focus on novel combustor concepts, his expertise extends across different areas including two-phase flow, experimental combustion, and laser flow diagnostics. Currently, his group is involved in the development of a novel gas turbine combustor capable of utilizing various fuels such as Hydrogen, methane, and SAF.

Dr. Shanmugadas is recognized for his contributions to fuel injection systems with many publications and his group has also filed many patents. He is actively engaged with industry and governmental bodies like ISRO and GE Aerospace and promotes impactful collaborations. He also works closely with the National Centre for Combustion Research and Development (NCCRD) at IIT Madras, focusing on advancing gas turbine combustion research. Additionally, he collaborates with the Chair of Space Propulsion and Mobility at Technical University Munich, Germany, in the area of injection concepts for liquid rocket engines.

Dr. Shanmugadas is a professional member of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) and a lifetime member of the Combustion Institute. He serves as the Associate Dean of Academic Affairs at IIT Jammu, contributing to the institution's academic excellence.

Dr. Shanmugadas is the founder of SAP Aerospace, a pioneering deep-tech startup focused on developing cutting-edge aero engines for Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicles (UCAVs).

##### Awards & honours Best paper award for the paper titled “Laser diagnostics techniques for the internal spray characterization of fuel injectors” at the National Conference on Future directions in Propulsion, held at LPSC, Thiruvananthapuram, India, 2018. Boeing BUILD 2.0 Innovation challenge winner, Development of a microgas turbine combustor technology for UCAV applications. Two PhD positions are available with our group. # 1. Development of low-emission combustor technology for micro gas turbine engines Our group has developed a novel gas turbine combustor concept that focuses on lean burn combustion to reduce emissions and improve efficiency. The objective of this project is to investigate the operational characteristics and performance of different injection systems based on Lean burn combustion concept. The candidate will conduct experimental investigations using advanced laser flow diagnostic techniques to quantify the flow and combustion processes. The proposed research involves detailed investigations with a radially staged gas turbine injectors to assess their fuel flexibility and performance characteristics. Interested candidates may contact the principal investigator (PI) for more details. The candidate is expected to have a basic degree in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering and a fundamental understanding of fluid and thermal systems. The candidate should also have good communication and writing skills and be able to work independently and in a team. # 2. Development of reusable liquid rocket engine combustors The rocket industry is pursuing the development of liquid rocket engines with rapid reusable capability. In this regard, our group has made advancements to develop a novel pintle injector concept for reusable methane-oxygen (METHALOX) liquid rocket engines. The objective of this project is to investigate the spray and combustion characteristics of this concept at subcritical operating conditions. The candidate will conduct experimental investigations using advanced optical and laser diagnostic techniques to quantify the spray and combustion processes. The proposed research involves detailed investigations of the effects of injector geometry, operating pressure, and fuel mixture ratio on the performance and stability of the pintle injector. Interested candidates may contact the PI for more details. The candidate is expected to have a basic degree in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering and a fundamental understanding of fluid and thermal systems. The candidate should also have good communication and writing skills and be able to work independently and in a team. **Junior Research Fellow** ISRO is developing high-capacity launch platforms for futuristic heavy duty launch vehicles. Our group is working in close collaboration with

ISRO to support various activities. The project involves detailed investigations on jet in free supersonic crossflow relevant to launch platforms. Rocket engine exhaust is cooled by injecting water in very high quantities to suppress the acoustic noise levels and to reduce the exhaust gas temperature. Physical models of the atomization processes and experimental databases are currently not available in the case of free supersonic crossflow configurations. The free supersonic gas stream structures and shock trails generated from a M=3 convergent-divergent nozzle will be characterized using focused color schlieren imaging, planar laser-induced fluorescence (TR-PLIF) imaging and Time-resolved particle imaging velocimetry (TR-PIV) experiments. Furthermore, predictive analytical models will be developed to correlate the droplet dispersion and spatial SMD with primary breakup parameters as a function of geometric and flow parameters. The candidate is expected to have a basic degree in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering and a fundamental understanding of fluid and thermal systems. The candidate should also have good communication and writing skills and be able to work independently and in a team. **Internship and B.Tech Project opportunities** Undergraduate, and postgraduate students interested in different domains of aerospace engineering are encouraged to apply for short-term engagements and internships. #### Rajkumar V

**Rajkumar Velu**, PhD, is an Assistant Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering and Dual Affilation: Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and worked as Visiting Assistant Professor at NTUST Taiwan in 2024. Priorly worked as a visiting research associate (2023) and Research Fellow at the Centre for Laser Aided Intelligent Manufacturing (CLAIM), University of Michigan, USA, and as a Post-Doctoral Research Fellow at the Digital Manufacturing and Design Centre (DManD), Singapore University of Technology and Design, Singapore. He completed his PhD thesis at the Auckland University of Technology, New Zealand, in selective laser sintering of specific biopolymer composites for biomedical application. Dr Velu is also the recipient of the prestigious GATE scholarship, best all-rounder award, best outgoing student gold medal for his academic performances at the university level. Consequently, he has considerable insights and knowledge on different polymer, hydrogels and composite materials used explicitly for the 3D printing process. He also has substantial experience in material and component characterization targeting specific responses, evaluating the complex thermo-mechanical aspects of powder bed processing, extrusion processing, and post-mortem studies for mechanical, electrical and biological attributes. More than 30 publications were generated from his research activities, and he received a scientific research award at an international conference conducted by Philadelphia University, Amman, Jordan 2010. Also, a recipient of the “young scientist award” 2018 at the Annual Conference on 3D printing and bioprinting in Health care, Singapore. Dr Velu’s research interests encompass engineering design, optimization, and developing advanced manufacturing processes (Additive Manufacturing) and materials for designing and fabrication for fuel cell, biomedical and aerospace applications. Moreover, based on his current research practice, he is involved in developing Artificial Intelligence into an additive manufacturing process to fabricate end-use products and eliminate post-mortem analysis.

##### Research Interest

Dr. Rajkumar Velu’s research is centered on additive manufacturing (AM) and advanced materials engineering, with a particular emphasis on the development, processing, and application of polymeric, metallic, and composite materials for high-performance and functional components. His multidisciplinary research integrates experimental and computational approaches to explore the following key areas:

1. Selective laser sintering of reactive polymers
2. Additive manufacturing of functionally graded structures
3. Fabrication of multifunctional components using continuous carbon fibre 3D printing
4. 3D bioprinting for biomedical and energy applications
5. SLM and Direct energy deposition of super alloys for aerospace applications

**B.Tech./B.E. Internships – Open**

Motivated undergraduate students from Mechanical, Materials, Metallurgy, Polymer, or Biomedical Engineering backgrounds with a strong academic record are invited to apply for research internships.

Please email your CV and academic transcripts with a brief note of interest.

Send your CV, transcripts, and a short cover letter outlining your research interests.

- Additive Manufacturing (polymers, metals, composites)
- Biomedical Implants, Bioprinting
- Aerospace, Automotive, and Energy Materials
- Computational Materials Science

Prior experience in experimental techniques or simulation tools (e.g., LAMMPS, FEA) is a plus.

Email your CV (with publications, if any) and a Statement of Purpose.

For application timelines and requirements, refer to theIIT Jammu Ph.D. admissions page.

## Dr. B. Satya Sekhar

#### Message

Welcome to the Department of Mechanical Engineering website.
The Department of Mechanical Engineering is one of the three founding departments at IIT
Jammu, which was established in August 2016. As of Summer 2024, the department comprises
twenty faculty members and six staff members and is home to about one hundred forty-four
undergraduate students and more than sixty-five graduate students. Our students come
from various states across the union of India and, thus, contribute to the department's
diversity.

The department houses well-equipped laboratories, which are
used for both instruction and research. Our faculty and research scholars are actively
involved in research in conventional and new Mechanical Engineering areas. Additionally,
we promote interdisciplinary research and collaborate with colleagues in other
departments, both within the institute and outside, including those in other countries,
to identify and work on research projects common to multiple branches of science and
engineering. Our major thrust areas in research include Applied Mechanics, Advanced
Manufacturing, Thermal Sciences, Renewable Energy, Nuclear Energy, and Compressible
Flows. The department is committed to excellence in teaching, research & development and
outreach related to science, engineering, technology development and policy matters
related to Mechanical Engineering

# Page Title: Mechanical Engineering | IIT Jammu

## ICCMDE-2026

The International Conference on Composite Materials, Design,
and Engineering (ICCMDE-2026) concluded successfully at Research and Innovation Park,
Indian Institute of Technology Delhi from 11–13 February 2026. Jointly organized
by Department of Mechanical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and
Department of Applied Mechanics, IIT Delhi under the Scheme for Promotion of Academic
and Research Collaboration (SPARC), the three-day event brought together leading
researchers, academicians, industry experts, and scholars to explore emerging
developments in composite materials, structural design, and advanced manufacturing
technologies.

The conference was inaugurated by Bent F. Sørensen,
Naresh Bhatnagar, R. T. Durai Prabhakaran, and Puneet Mahajan. It featured keynote talks
by eminent scientists including Naresh Bhatnagar (IIT Delhi), Bent F. Sørensen
(Technical University of Denmark), Anindya Deb (IISc Bengaluru), and Paul Hazell
(University of New South Wales, Australia), and **plenary talks by eminent
scientists including Russell J. Varley, RMIT, Melbourne, Australia, Giribaskar,
NMIS, UK, Saju Pillai (NIIST, Trivandrum), Raguraman (IIITDM Kancheepuram), Ramadass
(DRDO-RDE, Pune), Sanjay Palsule (IIT Roorkee), Inderdeep Singh (IIT Roorkee),
Rajnish Sharma (IIT Mandi), Pawan Kumar (CUJ, Jammu), Jayaramulu (IIT Jammu), Badri
Prasad Patel (IIT DELHI), S.P.Singh (IIT Delhi), Pradyumna (IIT Delhi), Abhijit
Majumdar (IIT Delhi), S Vikrant Karra (IIT Delhi), Naresh Datla (IIT Delhi), Dr.
Gautam Jain, Endolite India Limited, New Delhi, covering advanced composite
materials, structural performance, impact engineering, and emerging
applications.**

Participants engaged in high-quality technical sessions
spanning military-grade composites, biomedical innovations, advanced structural
materials, and next-generation manufacturing technologies, highlighting the
transformative role of composites in global engineering.

The valedictory session featured Professor Sawan S. Sinha,
Head - Department of Applied Mechanics, reflecting on key outcomes and emphasizing the
importance of continued interdisciplinary collaboration. Selected full-length papers
from ICCMDE-2026 will be published in a Special Issue of the Journal of Polymer
Composites, further extending the scholarly impact of the conference.

**Programs of Study Offered by the Department**

In this endeavour, we offer various undergraduate and postgraduate degrees and are actively
contemplating offering more diverse programs to cater to emerging educational trends. Some of
the programs we are currently offering are:

### B.Tech. in Mechanical
Engineering

The department offers a B.Tech. program in Mechanical
Engineering. This program
commenced in 2016 with a sanctioned strength of 30 students, which has now been
increased to 40. We offer many different specialization-related courses and minor
programs. Beginning from their third year, students work on B.Tech. projects to hone
their analytical abilities and acquire practical experience. The curriculum is
designed to expose undergraduate students to both theoretical and practical aspects
of their chosen discipline.

Apply Now

### M.Tech in Thermal and Energy
Systems

The M.Tech. programme in Thermal and Energy Systems
Engineering started in 2020 and
is designed for students with a background related to mechanical engineering. The
objective is to provide students with an organized curriculum consisting of courses
designed to enhance their capability to analyse and design innovative systems
requiring knowledge in thermal and energy sciences. Some of the focus areas include
energy conversion systems and their fuels, renewable energy, HVAC, nuclear energy
and fluid dynamics. Along with a practical orientation and fundamental knowledge,
the students also learn to simulate various fluid, thermal and energy systems using
different computational tools; this enhances their skill levels and makes them more
industry-ready.

Apply Now

### Ph.D. in
Mechanical Engineering

The Department of Mechanical Engineering offers a
terminal degree program, the Doctor
of Philosophy (Ph.D.), which was launched in 2018. This program is focused on three
broad areas of study: manufacturing, design, and thermo-fluid sciences. This
program, in which the research scholar’s financial needs are served by means of a
stipend, consists of a flexible curriculum of coursework relevant to the area of
study and research. These courses are chosen by the student in agreement with the
research adviser, followed by a significant emphasis on research culminating in a
dissertation. This PhD program is part of our effort to foster a world-class
technology and knowledge-driven society by training students who have a desire and
orientation towards a deeper understanding of scientific phenomena.

Apply Now
